rulings.Case Title: CHEN VS SETO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, November 21, 2024, line 1. DEFENDANT CHARLIE SETO's Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award. GRANTED. No opposition filed.= (501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: WINTERLAND SAN FRANCISCO PARTNERS, A CALIFORNIA VS. CINFONIE CHIU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Stipulation; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, November 21, 2024, line 10. PLAINTIFF WINTERLAND SAN FRANCISCO PARTNERS, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED PARTNERSHIP' Motion For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Stipulation. is Continued to December 18, 2024. No later than December 6, 2024 Plaintiff must file and serve a supplemental brief limited to citations to cases where Stipulation for Entry of Judgment (not an actual judgment) was enforced pursuant to CCP 128. CCP 128 is the only code section cited in the "Court Has Authority to Enter Judgment" section of the moving brief. If no such case authority is located, the brief shall state "no cases." No further argument shall be included in the brief. The brief shall not exceed 1 page. = (501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
